business.headline: 8th Pay Commission deadline extended again: What it means for your salary and arrearsAnother extension, more questions. The 8th Pay Commission has pushed back its submission deadline again, keeping employees guessing about pay hikes and arrears.

Will your home loan EMI rise again? What RBI's June MPC meet could mean for borrowersIf you have a home loan, the RBI's June policy meeting could be worth watching closely. Borrowers are eager to know whether their EMIs will remain stable or face another change in the months ahead.

Why is Nvidia hiring when the rest of Silicon Valley is firingNvidia is expanding H-1B hiring even as major technology rivals cut jobs and slow foreign recruitment. The move stands out for Indian professionals facing layoffs, visa pressure and a tighter US market.

Why your society maintenance bill could soon be Rs 3,000 higher every monthFor many apartment residents, the next surprise could arrive not in their power bill or fuel bill, but in their society maintenance statement. Rising wage costs and higher operating expenses are forcing several housing societies to consider significant fee hikes.

India-US trade talks begin today: Why Section 301 could become the biggest issueIndian and US negotiators have begun fresh trade talks in New Delhi with Section 301 emerging as a central issue. India wants assurance that future US tariff action will not erode gains from a bilateral trade deal.

TCS, Infosys, HCLTech jump up to 6%. 3 reasons why IT stocks are rallying today?IT stocks extended gains for a third straight session, led by Infosys, TCS, HCL Technologies and Tech Mahindra. The rally reflects stronger global tech sentiment, AI spending optimism, rate-cut hopes and support from a weaker rupee.

After Taiwan, South Korea overtakes India in global stock market rankingsSouth Korea has moved past India in global stock market rankings after a sharp rally in AI-linked chipmakers. The shift underlines how investors are favouring markets tied to the AI supply chain while Indian equities face near-term pressures.

Sensex opens 400 points lower, Nifty below 23,300; IT stocks rallyThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 302.16 points, or 0.41%, to 73,965.18 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 107.05 points, or 0.46%, to 23,275.55 as of 9:25 am.

Trump cuts tariffs on farm, industrial equipment from 25% to 15%. What it meansUS President Donald Trump has lowered US tariffs on selected farm and industrial equipment from 25% to 15% through December 2027. The move couples cheaper imports with incentives for greater use of American steel and aluminium.

Why LPG prices haven't risen despite global supply costs surgingIndian households continue to pay lower LPG rates even as global supply costs surge. The gap is being absorbed through subsidies and mounting under-recoveries at oil companies.

India may reconsider Scotch whisky tariff cuts if UK imposes steel curbsIndia could revisit tariff concessions granted to Britain on products such as Scotch whisky if London fails to address New Delhi's concerns over proposed steel restrictions, according to an Indian official.

Can small-cap funds really deliver higher long-term returns?While small caps have delivered better returns than large caps over the past two decades, the difference may be smaller than many investors expect, says a recent study.

SIP investing, the RCB way18 years. That's how long RCB supporters waited for glory. If patience eventually paid off in cricket, could the same principle be the secret behind successful SIP investing?

What is Section 301 of the US Trade Act? Why India wants protection from itAs negotiations enter the final stage, one issue attracting attention is India's effort to secure protection from possible US tariffs under Section 301 of the US Trade Act, 1974.

Piyush Goyal says 99% of India-US trade deal talks completedSpeaking about the ongoing negotiations, Goyal said discussions between the two sides would continue from June 2 to June 4 to resolve a few remaining issues before the agreement is formally announced.

Sensex sheds 500 points, Nifty slips below 23,400 despite IT rallyThe BSE Sensex fell 508.40 points, or 0.68%, to close at 74,267.34, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 165.15 points, or 0.70%, to settle at 23,382.60.

LPG prices, solar rules, tax and more : 5 big financial changes this JuneFrom LPG cylinder prices to tax payments, June brings a fresh set of financial changes for households and businesses. Some may increase costs, while others are aimed at improving security and supporting local manufacturing.

India tightens solar import rules from June 1. Here's what it means for consumersThe government now requires certain solar projects to use only domestically manufactured solar cells, a move aimed at reducing dependence on Chinese imports and strengthening local manufacturing.

US tightens curbs on Nvidia AI chip shipments to Chinese firms operating overseasThe US' best AI chips may have been making their way to the subsidiaries of Chinese AI firms based in places such as Malaysia despite broader US efforts to starve Chinese firms of semiconductors needed to develop critical AI capabilities, reported Reuters.

Have retail investors become the real gatekeepers of India's IPO market?The broader shift in India's equity markets from one that looked to foreign capital for validation to one anchored in domestic conviction has reached the primary market fully.

entertainment.headline: Caretaker held for stealing Rs 25 lakh from Raveena Tandon's brother's house Actor Raveena Tandon's family has been caught in a theft case after Juhu police arrested a woman for allegedly stealing jewellery and valuables from the residence of her brother, Rajiv Tandon.

Trisha shares witty post as link-up rumours with CM Vijay continue to fuel trolls Actor Trisha Krishnan shared an Instagram Story featuring her dog with a pointed caption. The post drew notice as online speculation around her equation with Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay continued.

Kamal Haasan production house video fuels Rajinikanth-Ashwath buzz. Watch Raaj Kamal Films International's birthday video for director Ashwath Marimuthu has reignited talk that he may direct Rajinikanth's Thalaivar 173. The buzz follows earlier changes in the film's direction team, though no official confirmation has been issued.

Why did Lalit Modi-Sushmita Sen break up? Businessman reveals the reason Businessman Lalit Modi has opened up on the reason behind his break-up with actor Sushmita Sen. He also defended her against the gold digger jibes, calling her self-made and generous.

Mika Abdalla, Jake Short end engagement amid Off Campus buzz Mika Abdalla and Jake Short have ended their engagement. The Off Campus actor's representative confirmed that the duo remain on friendly terms and have asked for privacy.

Kala Hiran is not Salman Khan's biopic: Producer Amit Jani on legal notice Amit Jani has called Salman Khan's legal notice against Kala Hiran premature and said the film is not a biopic on the actor. He said the film draws from public-domain events and foregrounds the Bishnoi community's link with wildlife conservation.

Pranit More's viral 'peak Gurgaon content' is actually peak rape-culture thinking The problem with the latest 'joke' on Pranit More's stand-up show is not just the fact that it was said, but how it was received: with applauses, laughter, amplification and glorification. Because buying a Rs 370 biryani is not the same as getting a woman's consent.

Chunnari Chunnari row grows as composers deny remake tag, call it homage The composers of Chunnari Chunnari - Let's Go say the song is a homage to the Biwi No 1 original, not a remake. The clarification comes amid social media backlash and a Rs 400 crore legal fight over song rights.

I grew up romanticising DDLJ's Raj. Off Campus just raised the bar As a 90s rom-com fan, Prime Video's Off Campus changed how I see screen romance. The male characters on the show listen, respect consent and honour boundaries, challenging the toxic behaviours many of us grew up accepting as normal in reel and real life.

Anushka-Virat seek Premanand Maharaj's blessings after RCB's IPL win. Pics Anushka Sharma and Virat Kohli visited Vrindavan to seek blessings from Premanand Maharaj. The visit comes after RCB's IPL title win for the second time.

Kim Kardashian nearly crashes her bike, Lewis Hamilton catches it on camera. Watch Kim Kardashian's latest Instagram carousel featured a playful New York bike ride clip filmed by Lewis Hamilton. The candid post has fired up talk that the pair are no longer keeping their romance fully under wraps.

After BAFTA win, Manipuri film Boong bags top honours at New York Film Festival Boong emerged as one of the top winners at the New York Indian Film Festival 2026, taking three awards. The result added to the Manipuri film's BAFTA milestone and underscored its continued international recognition.

Madhavan's birthday post features a rare cruise pic with Nayanthara, Trisha LIK director Vignesh Shivan shared throwback photos with Madhavan, Nayanthara and Trisha on the Dhurandhar actor's birthday. The photo is now going viral on social media.

Janhvi wins hearts with Telugu speech at Peddi event, recreates Sridevi's dialogue At Peddi's Vijayawada pre-release event, Janhvi Kapoor addressed the crowd in Telugu and thanked them for their love. The speech, along with her Manava Manava dialogue tribute to her late mother Sridevi, became a standout moment before the film's release.

Salman Khan sends legal notice to Kala Hiran makers, producer Amit Jani reacts Salman Khan's legal team has sent a notice to the makers of Kala Hiran, seeking to halt the film's release and promotions. Amit Jani has alleged intimidation, leaving the project's release timeline under scrutiny.

Kangana recalls mother's reaction to Gangster: Society won't accept this Kangana Ranaut said her parents were unimpressed after watching her debut film Gangster and focused on society's reaction to her role. She said their views changed after her National Award recognition.

No India-Pak mission in Alpha, Alia Bhatt's film is an assassin origin story Alia Bhatt's Alpha is being positioned as an assassin origin story in the YRF Spy Universe, not an India-Pakistan joint mission. The shift moves the film away from rumours about a possible mission involving two countries and towards a harder action-led narrative.

Vignesh Shivan to Lokesh Kanagaraj: Why are filmmakers blaming audience for flops? Vignesh Shivan's post on Love Insurance Kompany has renewed debate over filmmakers blaming audiences and online negativity after flops. The backlash has drawn parallels with comments by Lokesh Kanagaraj and others, sharpening questions over whether films simply failed to connect.

It's a Toy Story, and Taylor Swift said yes: Singer announces new song Taylor Swift has announced an original song for Disney and Pixar's Toy Story 5. Titled I Knew It, I Knew You, the track will release on June 5.

general news.headline: Final push that made Annamalai plan BJP exit, form new partyDynamic IPS officer-turned-politician K Annamalai is planning to quit the BJP and launch a party. While his exit might appear to be sudden, it comes amid a series of developments ahead of the 2026 Tamil Nadu Assembly elections. Here's what might have been the final push.

After delay, monsoon to reach Kerala on June 4; very heavy rainfall expectedThe weather agency has forecast widespread rainfall activity across the southern states over the next week, with Kerala expected to bear the brunt of the monsoon's arrival.

How a signature forgery row has pushed Trinamool to breaking pointA forged signature row over the Leader of the Opposition post has triggered expulsions and secret meetings in the Trinamool Congress. The controversy, which comes on the back of deepening dissent within the party, has intensified the buzz of a vertical split.

TCS, Infosys, HCLTech jump up to 6%. 3 reasons why IT stocks are rallying today?IT stocks extended gains for a third straight session, led by Infosys, TCS, HCL Technologies and Tech Mahindra. The rally reflects stronger global tech sentiment, AI spending optimism, rate-cut hopes and support from a weaker rupee.

I trained my binoculars on Virat Kohli right after the IPL final. I couldn't find himIPL 2026 final: From the press box in Ahmedabad, I had my binoculars trained on the middle, I waited for the Virat Kohli I knew. He didn't arrive. What showed up instead was something harder to achieve.

Siddaramaiah to retain CM bungalow, DK Shivakumar will move to another govt houseSiddaramaiah will continue in the Cauvery bungalow even as DK Shivakumar takes over as Chief Minister. The arrangement leaves the official residence unchanged while the new Chief Minister shifts to another government house.

Caught on camera: Witness in Twisha Sharma case attacked by 'husband's friends'Neeraj Dubey, who was made a witness in the Twisha Sharma death case, claimed that four to five men attacked him on May 30 near his beauty parlour in Bhopal's Katara Hills area and questioned why he was testifying in the high-profile case.

CBSE's Rs 384 crore OSM deal: How Coempt beat TCS by 2 marks and a 60% lower bidTender documents show Coempt Edu Teck beat TCS for CBSE's digital evaluation contract. The details have come under focus as CBSE's OSM platform faces criticism over delays and technical issues.

Anushka-Virat seek Premanand Maharaj's blessings after RCB's IPL win. PicsAnushka Sharma and Virat Kohli visited Vrindavan to seek blessings from Premanand Maharaj. The visit comes after RCB's IPL title win for the second time.

Live: TMC adopts wait-and-watch stance as expelled MLAs meet other party legislatorsBengal TMC crisis live updates: All eyes will be on TMC chief Mamata Banerjee today after she dared the Bengal government to arrest her, declaring that the party's protest would go ahead even without police permission, microphones or a stage. Meanwhile, the Bengal forged-signature row has snowballed after Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari said the probe stemmed from complaints by two TMC MLAs, who were later expelled. The crisis deepened further as the CID revisited Abhishek Banerjee's residence and claimed more legislators had disputed their signatures. Follow live updates.

Annamalai seeks meeting with Amit Shah amid BJP exit buzz, may launch own partySources said Annamalai has decided against accepting any position offered by the BJP, including potential central responsibilities or a Rajya Sabha berth. He has also ruled out joining any existing political party in Tamil Nadu, including TVK, and is instead preparing to launch an independent political outfit.

Do judges need so many breaks? A look at an SC judge's day to settle the debateThe Supreme Court has entered a six-week summer recess with only the vacation benches hearing urgent cases. The break has kickstarted the annual ritualistic debate over judicial vacations amid high pendency. Here's a look at an SC judge's day to settle the debate.

Pranit More's viral 'peak Gurgaon content' is actually peak rape-culture thinkingThe problem with the latest 'joke' on Pranit More's stand-up show is not just the fact that it was said, but how it was received: with applauses, laughter, amplification and glorification. Because buying a Rs 370 biryani is not the same as getting a woman's consent.

Now, NTA re-exam portal faces cyber security questions amid massive CBSE rowA 16-year-old cybersecurity researcher alleged vulnerabilities in the NTA's re-examination portal and possible unauthorised dashboard access. The claims, still unverified, have intensified concern after users reported the link showing a 404 error.

UP trader gets call from wife's phone, finds her with slit throat, severed earsAn open cupboard, a plate of sweets and two glasses of water have emerged as key clues in the killing of a Saharanpur woman. Investigators believe the victim may have let her attacker into the house, while police are probing whether an old personal rivalry triggered the murder.

3,400 deaths in a day: India's extreme heat days are turning deadlierOfficial counts of "heatstroke deaths" are often low, sometimes just a few hundred in a bad season, because many heat-related deaths are not labelled as such. This implies that the toll could be much higher.

Wrinkle-free careers: The curious rise of resume botoxProfessionals are trimming resumes to get past AI-led applicant tracking systems and avoid being seen as overqualified. The trend reflects rising concern over age bias and the limits of algorithm-driven hiring.

Watch: Glowing pillars of light seen falling from space over TibetA video recorded during a thunderstorm over Tibet shows towering red sprites flashing above the clouds. The rare upper-atmospheric event has drawn attention for both its visual drama and scientific value.

Can Mamata save her TMC, or will it slip out of her hands?The Trinamool Congress is facing an existential crisis with several leaders having left or on the verge of quitting. Will TMC supremo Mamata Banerjee be able to fight the desertions and planned defections, and rebuild the party, or will it slip away from her hands?

He battled jaundice twice and typhoid once, yet came out on top. Meet JEE AIR 3JEE Advanced 2026 AIR 3 Jatin Chahar's journey was far from smooth. The Rajasthan student battled jaundice twice and typhoid during preparation, yet secured a place among India's top three rankers. Here's how discipline, concept clarity, family support and a strict routine helped him get there.

One Wish Willow explained: The 'cursed' object from Obsession is going viralThe One Wish Willow from Obsession has become a viral talking point online as fans unbox replicas which are available to purchase as merchandise. The craze is being driven by the film's twisted wish premise and an immersive marketing campaign that makes the object feel real and worthy of possession.

Even plumbers and electricians are not safe from AI, says new study on jobsIn 2023, a report suggested that AI could disrupt 90 per cent of jobs by 2032. Now, 6 years later, it turns out that the prediction was not entirely accurate. AI did disrupt jobs, but in a much smaller timeframe than initially anticipated.

From young accused to frail convict: Bihar man, 84, found guilty in 1992 caseDuring the prolonged trial that spanned over three decades, four of the five accused died. The only surviving accused was Deep Rai, 84, who continued to face trial.

Caretaker held for stealing Rs 25 lakh from Raveena Tandon's brother's houseActor Raveena Tandon's family has been caught in a theft case after Juhu police arrested a woman for allegedly stealing jewellery and valuables from the residence of her brother, Rajiv Tandon.

sports.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026, Group B preview: Switzerland face tricky continental group FIFA World Cup 2026, Group B preview: Switzerland head a volatile Group B featuring Bosnia and Herzegovina, Canada and Qatar. The section will turn on veteran leaders, injury concerns and very little margin for error.

Man City's Rodri to decide future after World Cup as Real Madrid interest grows FIFA World Cup 2026: Rodri has parked questions over his Manchester City future until after Spain's World Cup campaign. His message to Spain is clear: work humbly, chase every ball, and ignore the noise.

After selection uproar, Auqib Nabi joins India's Test squad but with a twist IND vs AFG One-off Test: Auqib Nabi's omission from India's Test squad sparked widespread debate, but the BCCI has now handed the Jammu and Kashmir pacer an unexpected opportunity, offering a twist to the selection story.

I trained my binoculars on Virat Kohli right after the IPL final. I couldn't find him IPL 2026 final: From the press box in Ahmedabad, I had my binoculars trained on the middle, I waited for the Virat Kohli I knew. He didn't arrive. What showed up instead was something harder to achieve.

Roland Garros Day 10 Order of Play: Zverev and next-gen stars take centre stage Roland Garros moves into a loaded day with Alexander Zverev, Elena Svitolina and Joao Fonseca all on the schedule. The draw also carries a Ukrainian derby, a late-night clash of disruptors and Indian interest in boys doubles.

Virat Kohli, RCB fired up as Mo Bobat calls for unprecedented IPL feat IPL 2026: Royal Challengers Bengaluru ignited dreams of an unprecedented IPL three-peat after team director Mo Bobat delivered a stirring dressing-room rallying cry following their 2026 title victory over Gujarat Titans.

PV Sindhu wins Indonesia Open opener, potential An Se-young clash ahead PV Sindhu opened her Indonesia Open campaign with a straight-games win over Busanan Ongbamrungphan. The result sends her into the next round, where world No. 1 An Se-young is the likely challenge.

Franchise cricket's boom forces ICC to take action on jam-packed calendar The ICC has set up a committee to examine how the surge of franchise leagues can coexist with the international calendar. The move reflects growing concern that bilateral cricket is losing ground as players and boards follow the T20 money.

Arnaldi outlasts Tiafoe in 5-hour epic to seal historic Italian trio in Paris Roland Garros: Matteo Arnaldi outlasted Frances Tiafoe in a five-set French Open epic to reach the quarter-finals. The comeback completed a rare Italian trio in the last eight and set up a meeting with Matteo Berrettini.

Sabalenka and Osaka unite after Roland Garros clash, want women in prime-time slots Roland Garros 2026: Aryna Sabalenka and Naomi Osaka formed a united front at French Open, demanding more prime-time visibility for women's tennis after their historic fourth-round night clash ended a three-year shutout.

Rajat Patidar won titles, but Smriti Mandhana changed RCB first. Don't forget her Rajat Patidar helped Royal Challengers Bengaluru win their second IPL title on Sunday with a victory over Gujarat Titans. However, it is Smriti Mandhana who holds a special place in the franchise's history as the first RCB captain to win any title.

Sabalenka aces Michael Jackson's moonwalk after beating Osaka: VideoAryna Sabalenka beat Naomi Osaka 7-5, 6-3 to reach the French Open quarter-finals before breaking into a moonwalk on Court Philippe-Chatrier. The celebratory flourish capped another commanding display from the World No. 1.

Contract crisis hits ISL as futures of nearly 150 players hang in the balanceNearly 150 ISL players became free agents after their contracts expired amid the AIFF-club stand-off over next season. The ongoing uncertainty has weakened players' bargaining power and hampered clubs' preparations for the upcoming campaign.

Berrettini avenges Jannik Sinner, knocks Cerundolo out in straight setsItalian Matteo Berrettini avenged his compatriot Jannik Sinner in the French Open after beating young Juan Manuel Cerundolo on Monday, June 1. Cerundolo's impressive run in the tournament was stopped in the fourth round.

World Cup: South Africa squad departs without assistant coach after US visa delaySouth Africa's World Cup squad departed for Mexico on Monday, 24 hours later than scheduled, after visa complications disrupted the team's travel plans and left assistant coach Helman Mkhalele stranded in Johannesburg.

Thomas Tuchel backs England to overcome heat and humidity at FIFA World CupEngland manager Thomas Tuchel has backed his side to cope with the heat and humidity expected at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, insisting the challenging conditions in North America will be an obstacle to overcome rather than an excuse for underperformance.

Mischievous RCB stars pour ice-cold water on coach after IPL winRoyal Challengers Bengaluru celebrated their IPL win in style at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad on Sunday. RCB players Tim David and Salt emptied the entire ice bucket on head coach Andy Flower after the final.

Tim David suspended for RCB's IPL 2027 opener after ice-bag incident with umpireRoyal Challengers Bengaluru batter Tim David has been suspended for the franchise's opening match of IPL 2027 after breaching the league's Code of Conduct during the IPL 2026 final against Gujarat Titans at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad.

Serena Williams confirms shock tennis comeback at 44 for Queen's ChampionshipsSerena Williams has confirmed her return to professional tennis at the age of 44, nearly four years after her last competitive appearance. Williams has accepted a doubles' wildcard at the HSBC Championships at Queen's Club in London.

Balaji-Demoliner reach doubles quarters, Arnav Paparkar enters boys' last 16N. Sriram Balaji and Brazil's Marcelo Demoliner advanced to the men's doubles quarterfinals of the French Open on Monday, while Arnav Paparkar continued India's strong showing by reaching the boys' singles round of 16 in the junior competition.

Don't need to prove it to anybody: Satwik-Chirag just want Badminton to flourishSingapore Open 2026: Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty ended their two-year title wait by winning the Singapore Open. Their response afterwards was pointed: the win settled nothing for them, but renewed a call for sport to be recognised and supported properly.

French Open fines player Rs 71 lakh for sexist remark on refereeParaguayan tennis player Adolfo Vallejo has been fined 65,000 euros by the French Open after making sexist remarks about chair umpire Ana Carvalho. The controversy stems from his dramatic second-round defeat at Roland Garros.

FIFA World Cup 2026, Group A preview: Can Mexico finally end their quarterfinal jinx?FIFA World Cup 2026, Group A Preview: The stage is finally set for the FIFA World Cup 2026 with Group A kicking off things. Mexico start as the hot favourites but South Korea, Czechia and South Africa will be aiming to disrupt the party for the co-hosts.

ICC approves trial use of pink ball in Tests hit by bad lightThe usage of pink balls on a trial basis during Test matches in case bad light affects play, and allowing head coaches inside the field of play during drink breaks, are among the major changes to the playing conditions approved by the International Cricket Council (ICC) Board at its annual meeting in Ahmedabad.

ICC suspends Cricket Canada, national teams remain eligible for eventsThe International Cricket Council (ICC) suspended Cricket Canada with immediate effect for serious breaches of its membership obligations following the conclusion of its Board meetings in Ahmedabad.

Carlos Alcaraz enjoys beach vacation in Portugal amid injury layoff periodCarlos Alcaraz was spotted enjoying a beach getaway in Portugal while recovering from the wrist injury that has kept him out of major tournaments, including Roland Garros and Wimbledon.

RCB's dressing room is the biggest prize, not the IPLAndy Flower's tribute to Virat Kohli, Bhuvneshwar Kumar and Josh Hazlewood offered a glimpse into the culture behind RCB's back-to-back IPL titles, where leadership, trust and selflessness matter as much as talent.

PV Sindhu congratulates RCB on IPL glory, adds cheeky lucky charm remarkPV Sindhu congratulated Royal Challengers Bengaluru after their successful IPL title defence and joked that her move to Bangalore has coincided with their winning run. On Sunday, RCB beat Gujarat Titans in the final at the Narendra Modi Stadium.

Zee to broadcast FIFA World Cup 2026 in India, secures 8-year dealFIFA World Cup 2026: India was one of the last major markets without a World Cup broadcaster. That changed on Monday as Zee secured rights to the 2026 tournament and a host of FIFA events through 2034.

Senegal AFCON walkout sparks major law changes ahead of FIFA World Cup 2026FIFA World Cup: IFAB has approved sweeping law changes after AFCON 2026 flashpoints, including Senegal's final walkout and tougher sanctions for protest and confrontation. The measures will apply from 2026-27 and at World Cup 2026, signalling a harder line on dissent, delay and discriminatory conduct.

RCB's mane attraction: Rajat Patidar, like MS Dhoni is making calm the new cool againIPL 2026: The long hair may invite comparisons to MS Dhoni, but Rajat Patidar's calm leadership, trust in players and ability to stay composed under pressure are what truly make RCB's captain resemble Captain Cool.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
